title: The Frog Prince
lines: In times past, when wishing still helped one, there lived a king whose daughters were all beautiful, but the youngest was most beautiful of all.
One day the youngest princess was playing by a well in the forest when her golden ball fell into the water.
She began to weep because she could not reach it.
A frog appeared and said, 'Why do you weep so bitterly?'
The princess told him her trouble, and the frog offered to retrieve her ball if she would promise to be his friend and let him eat from her plate and sleep in her bed.
The princess agreed, thinking she would never see him again, but the frog jumped into the well and brought back her golden ball.
The next day the frog appeared at the palace gate.
The king commanded the princess to keep her promise.
The princess did not want to, but the frog ate from her plate and went to bed with her.
As she lay awake, she heard a strange sound.
When she looked, the frog was transforming into a handsome prince.
He explained that a wicked witch had changed him into a frog and that he had been waiting for a princess to keep her promise.
Her faithful promise had broken the spell.
The next morning they traveled to his kingdom, where they were received with great joy.
The prince and the princess were married and lived happily ever after.
